title: Preventing Unnecessary Re-renders in React Components

statement.markdown: A React developer wants to optimize the performance of a functional component by preventing unnecessary re-renders. However, the current implementation has a bug that causes the component to re-render unnecessarily.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a JSON object containing an array of numbers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the sum of the numbers in the array, but without re-rendering the component unnecessarily.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the array reference changes.

examples.explanation: The component should re-render and return the sum of the numbers in the array, which is 1 + 2 + 3 = 6.
The component should re-render and return the sum of the numbers in the array, which is 4 + 5 = 9.

hints.text: The component should only re-render when the array reference changes. You can use the `useMemo` hook to optimize the performance of the component.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Component Performance
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook is used to memoize the result of a function so that it is not recomputed unnecessarily.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Re-render the component on every state change.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the result of a function.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the result of a function and only re-render the component when the array reference changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `useMemo` hook ensures that the component only re-renders when the array reference changes, preventing unnecessary re-renders.
commonMistakes: Not checking for array reference changes
